articles.title: Heatwave In Vidarbha: Amravati And Wardha Hottest In Country - The Times of India
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Maharashtra
articles.full_text:
Nagpur: Vidarbha continued to reel under an intense heatwave on Saturday, with Amravati and Wardha jointly recording the highest maximum temperature in the country at 46 degrees Celsius, according to
India Meteorological Department data. Seven cities from Vidarbha featured among the 10 hottest places in India, underlining the severity of the ongoing heatwave across the region.
Apart from Amravati and Wardha, Akola recorded 45.4 degrees Celsius, Brahmapuri and Yavatmal touched 45.2 degrees Celsius each, while Nagpur registered 43.8 degrees Celsius. Chandrapur recorded 43.6 degrees Celsius. The IMD stated that maximum temperatures across most parts of Central India remained in the 40-45 degrees Celsius range, and a yellow alert has been issued for Vidarbha for the next four days.
Senior pulmonologist Dr Ashok Arbat said that the return of the extreme dry winds (called loo in Hindi) has sharply increased heat-related emergencies. "Hospital admissions linked to dehydration, heat exhaustion and sunstroke increased significantly compared to the same period last year. Many patients arrive in critical condition, unconscious or severely dehydrated, though most cases are preventable," he said.
Dr Arbat cautioned that heat-related illnesses can progress rapidly from fatigue, cramps to life-threatening emergencies. Sunstroke, where body temperature crosses 40 degrees and patients stop sweating, is considered medical emergency. Severe dehydration can trigger cardiac complications.
Children, senior citizens, pregnant women, outdoor workers and patients suffering from asthma, COPD, diabetes, kidney and heart diseases are among the most vulnerable groups.

articles.full_text:
Maharashtra Weather Update: IMD Issues Heatwave, Thunderstorm Alert Across State; Rain Likely in Several Districts
By Lokmat Times Desk | Updated: May 17, 2026 08:31 IST2026-05-17T08:30:38+5:302026-05-17T08:31:46+5:30
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast a combination of heatwave conditions, hot and humid weather, and thunderstorm activity ...
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast a combination of heatwave conditions, hot and humid weather, and thunderstorm activity across various parts of Maharashtra over the next five days, according to its latest district-wise weather bulletin.
Mumbai, Palghar and several areas of north Maharashtra are expected to experience mainly dry weather conditions until May 20. However, isolated hot and humid conditions are likely in Thane and Raigad districts on Saturday before the weather turns dry in the coming days.
In Mumbai, the Colaba observatory recorded a maximum temperature of 34.3 degrees Celsius, while Santacruz registered 34.1 degrees Celsius. No rainfall activity was reported in the state on Saturday.
Meanwhile, the IMD has predicted thunderstorms accompanied by lightning, moderate rainfall and gusty winds ranging from 30 to 50 kmph in parts of Konkan, western Maharashtra and Marathwada.
Ratnagiri and Sindhudurg districts are likely to witness thunderstorms and moderate rainfall over the next two days, followed by light to moderate showers and thundershowers till May 20. Ratnagiri recorded a maximum temperature of 34.1 degrees Celsius on Saturday.
Several districts in western Maharashtra, including Kolhapur, Satara, Sangli and Solapur, have also been placed under thunderstorm alert with chances of gusty winds reaching up to 50 kmph at isolated locations.
Pune, Ahilyanagar and the ghat regions of Pune are expected to receive light to moderate rainfall and thundershowers between May 16 and May 18. Pune’s Shivajinagar station recorded 39.2 degrees Celsius, which was 2.3 degrees above normal.
According to the IMD, Pune is likely to witness partly cloudy skies by Sunday afternoon or evening, with temperatures expected to hover around 38 degrees Celsius maximum and 24 degrees Celsius minimum.
In Marathwada, districts including Beed, Nanded, Latur and Dharashiv are likely to experience thunderstorms, lightning and moderate rainfall over the next three days. The weather department has advised residents to remain cautious during thunderstorm activity and avoid taking shelter near trees or electrical structures.
On Saturday, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar recorded 41.6 degrees Celsius, while Parbhani touched 44 degrees Celsius.
Heatwave conditions are expected to continue in Vidarbha districts such as Akola, Amravati, Nagpur and Wardha until May 20. The highest temperature in the state was recorded at 46 degrees Celsius in Amravati and Wardha.
The IMD has urged people to stay hydrated, avoid prolonged exposure to heat, and take precautions while stepping outdoors during peak afternoon hours.
The department has also issued an advisory for farmers, urging them to complete harvesting of mature crops and safely store produce due to the possibility of unseasonal rain and strong winds in several regions.

articles.title: Maharashtra Heatwave: Heatwave Continues in the State; Higher Temperatures in Vidarbha, Marathwada, and North Maharashtra - Lokshahi English News
articles.full_text:
Maharashtra Heatwave: Heatwave Continues in the State; Higher Temperatures in Vidarbha, Marathwada, and North Maharashtra
Despite the sounds of monsoon in the Arabian Sea and Andaman region, the heatwave situation persists in Maharashtra. At the same time, conducive conditions for pre-monsoon stormy rains have also developed, leading the weather department to issue alerts for some areas. Today (17th), there is a possibility of a heatwave in Marathwada and Vidarbha, while stormy rains are forecasted for Konkan, central Maharashtra, and southern districts of Marathwada.
On Saturday (16th), the highest temperature in the country was recorded at 45.6 degrees Celsius in Amaravati during the previous 24 hours until morning. The weather department reported that a low-pressure area is currently active in the southwest Bay of Bengal, and a cyclonic wind pattern has developed up to about 4.5 kilometers above sea level. During the same period, temperatures exceeding 45 degrees were recorded in Amaravati, Akola, Wardha, and Nagpur, while more than 43 degrees were noted in Yavatmal, Brahmapuri, and Chandrapur, and maximum temperatures above 42 degrees were recorded in Dhule, Jalgaon, Malegaon, Parbhani (general and agriculture), Bhandara, Gadchiroli, and Washim. Meanwhile, Mahabaleshwar recorded the lowest temperature in the state at 20.9 degrees Celsius.
According to the weather department's forecast, a severe heatwave is expected in the districts of Akola, Amaravati, Wardha, and Nagpur in Vidarbha today, while there is a possibility of stormy rains in Ratnagiri-Sindhudurg, Kolhapur, Solapur, Beed, Dhule, and Latur; hence a 'yellow alert' has been announced for these areas. The rest of the state will remain partially cloudy, and there is a possibility of rain with lightning in isolated places, while some areas may experience heat stress during the afternoon.
The weather department urges citizens to take precautions related to heat and stormy rains and to follow official instructions when necessary. The department has also requested farmers to remain cautious regarding crop concepts according to the weather.
